policy.title: RBI releases final guidelines on Climate Risk and Sustainable Finance for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI mandates climate stress testing for all scheduled commercial banks with assets over ₹1 lakh crore by March 2027, requiring PD adjustments for physical risk exposure. Banks must establish dedicated climate risk management committees and submit quarterly transition risk disclosures.

policy.title: SEBI expands BRSR Core to top 500 listed companies, introduces supply chain Scope 3 mandate
policy.description: SEBI's updated BRSR Core framework now requires reasonable assurance on Scope 1-2 emissions and limited assurance on Scope 3 for the top 500 companies by market cap, effective FY2026-27. New biodiversity and water stress metrics added as mandatory indicators.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI issues exposure draft on parametric insurance product guidelines for climate perils
policy.description: Draft guidelines permit insurers to design index-based products for flood, drought, and cyclone perils using approved government or third-party weather data providers. Maximum payout limits set at ₹50 crore per event for commercial policies.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee finalizes Pillar 3 climate risk disclosure framework for banks
policy.description: The framework requires internationally active banks to disclose physical and transition risk exposures by sector, geography, and maturity starting January 2027. Standardized templates for financed emissions and climate-sensitive asset concentrations are now mandatory.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced granularity for emerging markets
policy.description: Updated scenarios include India-specific GDP impact pathways under 1.5°C, 2°C, and NDC scenarios through 2050. New variables include monsoon variability indices and agricultural productivity shocks at state-level resolution.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

policy.title: European Commission adopts sector-specific ESRS for financial institutions under CSRD
policy.description: Banks and insurers operating in EU must report on climate risk transmission channels, green asset ratios, and taxonomy-aligned lending by January 2027. Non-EU subsidiaries with EU revenues over €150 million are in scope.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence closes $100M Series D to expand physical risk analytics in Asia-Pacific
competitors.description: Funding led by TPG Rise Climate will support expansion into India and Southeast Asia, with plans to establish a Mumbai analytics hub by Q3 2026. Company claims 40% of global top-50 banks now use its ClimateScore platform.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory partners with SBI to develop natural capital risk assessment framework
competitors.description: Partnership will create India's first bank-level biodiversity risk scoring methodology, covering SBI's ₹35 lakh crore loan book. Pilot assessment of 500 corporate borrowers expected by September 2026.

competitors.title: Moody's launches Climate Pathway Alignment tool with India sector modules
competitors.description: New product assesses corporate transition readiness against IEA Net Zero pathways with dedicated modules for Indian power, steel, and cement sectors. Integrates with existing credit rating workflows for 1,200+ Indian issuers.

competitors.title: Sprih raises $8M Series A, launches BRSR automation platform for mid-cap companies
competitors.description: Round led by Omnivore with participation from Better Capital targets expansion beyond enterprise clients. New product automates 85% of BRSR Core data collection with built-in assurance workflows.

competitors.title: XDI partners with CRISIL to integrate physical risk scores into Indian infrastructure ratings
competitors.description: Partnership embeds XDI's asset-level physical risk analytics into CRISIL's infrastructure credit ratings covering roads, ports, and power assets. Coverage includes 2,500+ infrastructure projects across India.

competitors.title: Watershed launches financial services module with portfolio financed emissions calculator
competitors.description: New module enables banks and asset managers to calculate PCAF-aligned financed emissions across asset classes. Claims 50% reduction in data collection time through automated counterparty emissions estimation.

research.title: Climate Risk and Bank Lending in Emerging Markets: Evidence from India
research.description: Study of 45 Indian banks finds physical risk exposure increases NPAs by 12-18 basis points per standard deviation of flood/drought intensity. Recommends mandatory climate-adjusted provisioning for agricultural and MSME portfolios.

research.title: Parametric Insurance Solutions for Climate Resilience in South Asia
research.description: Report identifies $14 billion annual protection gap for climate perils in India, with parametric products best suited for cyclone (Maharashtra, Odisha) and flood (Bihar, Assam) corridors. Basis risk can be reduced to under 8% with 5km-resolution precipitation data.

research.title: Bridging the ESG Data Gap: Emerging Market Disclosure Quality Assessment 2026
research.description: Analysis of 2,400 emerging market companies finds only 23% meet ISSB-equivalent disclosure standards, with Indian firms outperforming at 34%. Data gaps most acute in Scope 3 emissions, water stress, and supply chain traceability.

research.title: Physical Risk Transmission to Credit Risk: A Framework for Supervisors
research.description: Framework provides methodology for translating physical hazard scenarios into PD/LGD adjustments across 12 sectors. Suggests Indian banks face 2.1-3.8% increase in expected credit losses under RCP 4.5 by 2030.

research.title: Climate Stress Testing: Progress and Challenges for Indian Banks
research.description: Survey of 25 Indian banks reveals 72% lack internal physical risk assessment capabilities and rely on external vendors. Average bank can model only 35% of portfolio at asset-level granularity required by RBI draft guidelines.

research.title: Index-Based Insurance and Agricultural Credit: Lessons from PMFBY Implementation
research.description: RBI working paper finds parametric weather triggers reduce moral hazard and claims processing time by 60% versus indemnity products. Recommends banks integrate parametric coverage as credit enhancement for priority sector agricultural lending.

ourRead.headline: RBI's climate stress testing mandate creates an 18-month window for StepChange to become the default physical risk infrastructure for Indian banks.
body: This week's signals converge on a single theme: the regulatory architecture for climate risk in Indian financial services is crystallizing faster than banks can build internal capacity. The RBI's final guidelines (policy-1), NGFS Phase V scenarios with India-specific pathways (policy-5), and CRISIL's finding that 72% of banks lack physical risk capabilities (research-5) together define an urgent, addressable market gap.

The RBI guidelines are transformational. Banks over ₹1 lakh crore must conduct climate stress tests by March 2027—just 12 months away—with PD adjustments tied to physical risk exposure. The World Bank's research (research-1) quantifying 12-18 bps NPA impact per unit of hazard intensity provides the empirical foundation StepChange needs to validate our PD/LGD models. We should immediately align our methodology to NGFS Phase V India scenarios and position our platform as the bridge between NGFS macro pathways and asset-level credit risk.

The competitive landscape is heating up, but differentiation opportunities remain clear. Jupiter's $100M raise and India expansion (comp-1) signals validation of the physical risk analytics market, but their global platform will struggle with India-specific hazards like monsoon variability. More concerning is XDI's CRISIL partnership (comp-5) embedding physical risk into infrastructure ratings—this threatens our distribution through the rating agency channel. However, neither addresses parametric insurance design, where IRDAI's draft guidelines (policy-3) and Swiss Re's $14 billion protection gap analysis (research-2) create a blue ocean for StepChange's second strategic bet.

The IFC's emerging market ESG data study (research-3) validates our global south data thesis: at 23% ISSB-compliance rates, the data infrastructure gap is structural, not temporary. Indian firms at 34% compliance creates a relative advantage we can monetize with global investors seeking EM exposure. The CRISIL survey's finding that banks can only model 35% of portfolios at required granularity is our sales pitch in a single statistic.

Watch for IRDAI's final parametric guidelines in the next 60 days—early engagement with insurers designing climate peril products could establish StepChange as the trigger-data provider of record. Action item: schedule meetings with the top 5 private insurers before final guidelines drop, armed with Swiss Re's basis risk findings showing sub-8% error rates are achievable with high-resolution precipitation data we can provide.
